section.name: Revocation Management
section.t: Revocation provides the kill switch for agent identity. When an agent is revoked, all its Credential Tokens become invalid and the agent can no longer act on behalf of any principal.

section.name: Revocation Object
section.t: Revocation is performed by submitting a signed Revocation Object to POST /v1/revocations.

tr.td: Invalidates specific scopes for the agent. For Tier 1 and 2, this MUST cause Relying Parties to reject Credential Tokens containing the revoked scopes (Step 7, Section 9).
Invalidates delegation chains rooted at target. Child agents lose authority.
Issued by the root principal to revoke their entire authorisation of the target agent (via AID) or all agents under their authority (via Principal DID).

section.name: Certificate Revocation List (CRL)
section.t: The Registry MUST expose a CRL at GET /v1/crl. The CRL MUST be updated within 15 minutes of a new Revocation Object being accepted. The CRL endpoint MUST be served from a CDN or distributed infrastructure.

section.name: Revocation Checking
section.t: A token's Tier is determined by its highest-risk scope. A token with one Tier 2 scope and any number of Tier 1 scopes is a Tier 2 token.
Tier 1 - Bounded-staleness: TTL is 3600 seconds. Validate against CRL at issuance time. CRL MUST be refreshed every 15 minutes.

Tier 2 - Real-time revocation: For scopes: transactions.*, communicate.*, filesystem.execute, spawn_agents.create, spawn_agents.manage. TTL is 300 seconds. Real-time Registry check on EVERY request. MUST NOT cache revocation status. DPoP MUST be verified. If Registry unreachable: MUST deny and return registry_unavailable.
Tier 3 - Enterprise: MUST use mTLS. MUST support OCSP per RFC 6960. Tier 3 supplements, not replaces, Tier 2.
Child Agent Propagation: When the Registry processes a Revocation Object with propagate_to_children: true, the Registry MUST recursively revoke descendants within 15 seconds.
Replica Registries MUST synchronise within 45 seconds. Combined end-to-end propagation MUST NOT exceed 60 seconds.

Approval Envelope interaction: When an agent AID is revoked, the Registry MUST transition all Approval Envelopes in pending_approval, approved, or executing status to failed. Unclaimed steps for that actor MUST be marked failed. In-progress claims MUST be treated as failed; the Registry MUST initiate compensation if applicable.

section.name: Registry Push Notification Protocol (RPNP)
section.t: RPNP is an OPTIONAL Registry capability for real-time revocation event delivery.

section.name: Overview

ul.li: Registry MUST deliver push events within 5 seconds
Push payloads MUST be signed by the Registry's administrative key
Subscriber authentication MUST be verified at subscription time

section.t: For subscribing Relying Parties, the effective revocation window is the RPNP delivery latency (at most 5 seconds) rather than the CRL refresh interval. RPNP does not replace CRL; it supplements it.

section.name: Subscription
section.t: A Relying Party subscribes by calling POST /v1/subscriptions:

tr.td: REQUIRED when scope_filter is aid or principal

section.t: The subscription request MUST be authenticated via DPoP proof. Un-signed requests MUST be rejected with subscription_auth_required.

section.name: Push Event Payload
section.t: Push events are delivered as HTTP POST to the subscriber's webhook_uri. The body is a compact-serialised JWT signed by the Registry's administrative key.
The request MUST include an X-AIP-Signature header containing HMAC-SHA256(shared_secret, body). The subscriber MUST verify both the JWT signature and the HMAC.

ol.li: Registry MUST deliver push events within 5 seconds
On delivery failure: retry with exponential backoff (1s, 2s, 4s - 3 attempts minimum)
After 3 consecutive failures: mark subscription as degraded. Subscriber falls back to CRL.
Subscribers MUST reject duplicate jti within 60 seconds
